latest.title: Dotdigital Loyalty and new AI capabilities
latest.content:
16 Jul 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 5380M
dotDigital Group plc
16 July 2026
16 July 2026
Dotdigital Group plc
("Dotdigital" or the "Group")
Launch of Dotdigital Loyalty and new AI capabilities
Extending the Group's customer lifecycle proposition and revenue opportunity
Dotdigital Group (AIM: DOTD), the leading SaaS provider of an AI-powered customer experience and data platform for intelligent personalised marketing engagement at scale, announces the launch of Dotdigital Loyalty, a new loyalty product initially developed for Shopify merchants, and Agents in Dotdigital, a natural language prompting interface embedded in core platform workflows.
Both products are now live and available to customers, alongside the Group's recently launched Dotdigital MCP server, which connects the platform to a customer's large language model of choice.
Together, the launches extend the Group's customer lifecycle proposition, deepen the AI capability embedded across the platform, and increase the revenue opportunity within Dotdigital's existing customer base.
Dotdigital Loyalty
Dotdigital Loyalty enables merchants to build and manage loyalty programmes natively within the Dotdigital platform, connecting loyalty data with customers' wider marketing campaigns to support more personalised engagement across touchpoints. Functionality includes transactional and behavioural earning, flexible rewards, tiered programmes, points management, and analytics, with native Dotdigital and Shopify integrations. Development for additional ecosystem partners is planned.
The product extends a connected customer lifecycle suite spanning acquisition, retention, personalisation and advocacy: Alia acquires and enrols customers, Dotdigital Loyalty retains and rewards them, Dotdigital Personalization (formerly Fresh Relevance) personalises touchpoints, and Social Snowball turns loyal customers into growth channels. This creates cross-sell opportunities across the Group's customer base and strengthens the strategic rationale for its recent acquisitions.
Loyalty is an established and growing category of marketing spend, and its addition expands the share of customer marketing budgets addressable by the Group.
Agents in Dotdigital
Agents in Dotdigital is a prompting interface added to core in-platform workflows, including campaign creation and segment creation, enabling marketers to perform complex actions using natural language. The feature is designed to reduce time-to-execution for customers and deepen platform engagement.
Dotdigital MCP server
The recently launched MCP server connects Dotdigital to a customer's large language model of choice, such as ChatGPT or Claude. Users can manage contact information, retrieve campaign data and access Dotdigital insights, including campaign performance benchmarked against industry peers using aggregated and anonymised data. The MCP server positions Dotdigital's platform within customers' emerging AI-driven workflows.
Milan Patel, CEO of Dotdigital, commented
:
"With Dotdigital Loyalty now live, we offer marketers a connected proposition across the full customer lifecycle. Combined with Agents in Dotdigital and our MCP server, these launches deepen the AI capability across our platform, strengthen our differentiation, and expand our revenue opportunity across the brands we serve."
Contacts
Dotdigital Group plc
+44 (0)20 3953 3072
Milan Patel, CEO
investorrelations@dotdigital.com
Tom Mullan, CFO
Alma Strategic Communications

Sunila de Silva, Equity Capital Markets
Singer Capital Markets (Joint Broker)

Shaun Dobson
Jen Boorer
About Dotdigital
Dotdigital Group plc (AIM: DOTD) is a leading provider of cross-channel marketing automation technology to marketing professionals. Dotdigital's customer experience and data platform (CXDP) combines the power of automation and AI to help businesses deliver hyper-relevant customer experiences at scale. With Dotdigital, marketing teams can unify and enrich their customer data, identify valuable customer segments, and deliver personalised cross-channel customer journeys that result in engagements, conversions, and loyalty.
Founded in 1999, Dotdigital is headquartered in London with offices in Manchester, Southampton, New York, Melbourne, Sydney, Singapore, Tokyo, Warsaw and Cape Town. Dotdigital's solutions empower over 4,000 brands across 150 countries.

latest.title: New Website
latest.content:
1 Jul 2026 08:00
RNS Number : 5642K
dotDigital Group plc
01 July 2026
1 July 2026
Dotdigital Group plc
("Dotdigital" or the "Group")
New Website
Dotdigital Group (AIM: DOTD), the leading SaaS provider of an AI-powered customer experience and data platform for intelligent personalised marketing engagement at scale, announces the launch of its new website alongside a refresh of the Dotdigital commercial brand.
The new corporate website is accessible at
https://dotdigital.com/
. Investor relations and AIM Rule 26 information will now be accessible at
investors.dotdigital.com
.
The website launch coincides with the Group's commercial rebrand and reflects Dotdigital's evolution into an all-in-one customer experience and data platform, supported by sustained investment in AI, data and personalisation and the acquisitions of Fresh Relevance, Social Snowball and Alia Software. Following a global, digital launch today, the new branding and product roadmap will be presented at
Dotdigital Summit
on 2 July, the Group's flagship customer event.
Milan Patel, CEO of Dotdigital, commented:
"
Our new brand and website are a clear expression of our market positioning and the value we deliver to more than 9,800 customers globally. We remain focused on maintaining the pace of product innovation, with further enhancements to come as we continue to expand the capability of the platform through our AI-focused roadmap."

company_30s.what_they_do: Dotdigital offers cloud‑based digital‑marketing software that lets brands create, send and analyse email, SMS and social‑media campaigns. It also builds marketing automation workflows that tie customer data to personalised offers.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global digital‑marketing market is worth tens of billions of pounds. Dotdigital positions itself against larger rivals like Marketo and HubSpot by focusing on mid‑market UK and EU clients, offering a cheaper, more flexible platform. Its AI‑driven recommendation engine promises to increase campaign ROI, a key pain point for marketers.
company_30s.current_state: The company is a mid‑cap AIM issuer that has recently refreshed its product line with AI features and acquired a small automation vendor (Alia). It is still in a growth phase, operating on a cash‑burn model but showing signs of stabilising revenue streams. The share trades around 45p, far below its all‑time high.

rns_narrative: Dotdigital is pivoting its core product around AI, launching new loyalty and recommendation modules. The acquisition of Alia expands its automation capabilities, while a new website signals a brand refresh.
Corporate governance updates – a new Chief Revenue Officer and director shareholding changes – suggest a tightening of management focus.
Financial announcements indicate that the company is still unprofitable but is tightening costs and preparing for a Q2 earnings release that could reveal a turnaround.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet but cautious optimism – a handful of traders see upside in the AI push, while others remain wary of the company's earnings track record.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish chatter highlights buying at 45p and bets that the AI launch will unlock new revenue streams. Some posts mention a potential 50% jump to 70p if the product gains traction.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the company’s low current earnings, dilution risk from share issuances, and the uncertainty around whether AI will truly differentiate it from larger competitors.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has been flat for weeks, but recent bullish posts around the AI announcement hint at a possible shift. The overall mood remains subdued, with few traders actively discussing the stock.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is dead, meaning there is no mainstream chatter. This lack of noise is a contrarian green light – a surprise catalyst could hit the market with little prior warning.

near_term_catalysts: The official launch of the new AI‑powered loyalty and recommendation engine – a press release or product demo could spark a price move.
The Q2 earnings report, if it shows a positive revenue surprise or a clear path to profitability.
Potential partnership announcements with a large UK retailer or a new enterprise customer that would validate the platform’s value.
A volume spike of at least 3× the daily average during a price breakout, indicating institutional buying.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals: a 10‑day moving‑average break to the upside, a 30‑minute chart spike in volume, and any positive commentary from the CEO or product team. If the share price climbs 10% from its current level on high volume, that would be a strong confirmation to enter.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a recovery cycle. The APEX score suggests a decent level of compression, but the market has not yet priced in the AI catalyst. A mean‑reversion play would need to wait for clear volume support and a bullish break of the 20‑day average.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the AI launch drives even modest revenue growth, the share could recover to mid‑30s pence, delivering roughly 70% upside from today’s level. The historical mean reversion of similar AI‑driven tech names has produced 50–100% gains when a catalyst is hit.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company’s current lack of earnings and the risk of dilution from share issuances could push the price lower. A negative earnings surprise or a failure to secure a major client could result in a 30% drop.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside of 70% versus downside risk of 30% gives a risk‑reward ratio of about 2.3:1 – a favourable asymmetry for a contrarian position.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a breakout above 48p with volume 3× the daily average. Use a 2% position size of your trading capital. Place a stop 4p below the entry (around 44p) to cap losses.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the Q2 earnings release or the AI product launch. If the share rises 10% from its current level on high volume, consider a partial entry at 50p.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable – no imminent delisting or bankruptcy risk has been identified.

ai_insights.bottom_line: DOTD.L sits on a promising AI pivot with little public interest; if the launch resonates, a contrarian long could capture a 70% upside with a 30% downside, making it an attractive asymmetric play.
